item.title: resolve_backlog_file returns wrong file for IDs that don't exist in expected file
item.intent: Ensure resolve_backlog_file returns an error when an ID is not found in its expected file, not a stale filename
item.description: resolve_backlog_file() uses a prefix case statement: FEAT-* maps to backlog.json, BUG-* maps to bugs.json. It returns the filename immediately without verifying the item actually exists in that file. If find_item then calls jq on the returned filename, it gets an error like 'null' rather than a clear 'item not found'. This is latent: when a FEAT-* item is present only in a custom file, or after backlog clean removes it, resolve_backlog_file returns a stale path and find_item produces a confusing error. The fix is to verify item existence in the resolved file before returning, or at minimum ensure find_item handles the null case gracefully.
steps: For the FEAT-* and BUG-* prefix cases, add a jq existence check after resolving the filename: if jq -e --arg id "$id" '.items[] | select(.id == $id)' "$BACKLOG_DIR/$file" &>/dev/null; then echo "$file"; return 0; fi
Fall through to the generic search-both-files loop if the prefix-matched file doesn't contain the item
Verify: resolve_backlog_file BUG-999 (nonexistent) returns non-zero; resolve_backlog_file BUG-001 still returns bugs.json
acceptanceCriteria: resolve_backlog_file returns non-zero for an ID that doesn't exist in any file, even if the prefix matches
resolve_backlog_file still returns the correct file for existing FEAT-* and BUG-* items
find_item error message is 'Item not found: <ID>' not a jq null error when the item is absent
